expectations.text: Follows the user's pivot to CI pipeline without resistance
expectations.evidence: The response immediately engages with the CI pipeline topic ('40 minutes is painful but not unusual') with zero mention of deployment strategy or any attempt to redirect back to the original topic.

expectations.text: Does not try to steer back to deployment strategy
expectations.evidence: Deployment strategy is never mentioned in the response. The entire reply is focused exclusively on CI pipeline performance.

expectations.text: Engages substantively with the CI pipeline problem
expectations.evidence: The response provides substantive engagement: challenges the user's assumptions ('Most teams I've seen blame the wrong thing'), offers a structured breakdown of where time goes (checkout, build, tests, artifacts), raises the p50 vs p95 distinction, and asks about CI platform constraints.

expectations.text: Asks diagnostic questions about what makes the pipeline slow
expectations.evidence: Multiple diagnostic questions asked: (1) breakdown across checkout/build/test/artifact steps, (2) whether build is incremental or full, (3) which test suite is heaviest, (4) whether 40 min is p50 or p95, (5) what CI platform is used. These are pointed, specific diagnostic questions rather than generic ones.
